description: Sector overlay for French electronic-communications operators, internet access providers, mobile/fixed telecom services, network operators, consumer protection, cybersecurity, and service-quality documentation.

metadata.scope: France-only sector overlay controls for Telecoms. Intended to be used with france_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official French/EU legal, regulatory, supervisory, and guidance sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: Arcep consumer-protection role and operator regulation

rules.title: Arcep operator, numbering, coverage, or service-obligation evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for Arcep, electronic-communications operator, numbering, coverage commitments, universal service, or network authorization evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this sector control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified France compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, current legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Telecom consumer protection or complaint evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for portability, net neutrality, complaints, accessibility, service quality, cancellation, or consumer information evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this sector control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified France compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, current legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: Arcep consumer-protection role and operator regulation

rules.title: Network security or incident-response evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for network security, incident response, resilience, access control, vulnerability management, or cybersecurity governance evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this sector control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified France compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, current legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Subscriber, traffic, or customer-data privacy evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for subscriber data, traffic data, location data, GDPR/RGPD, retention, privacy notice, or data-subject rights evidence.
